items.title: Dow closes at record 51,999.67; S&P 500 and Nasdaq fall as tech rotation intensifies
items.content: Dow +0.64% to 51,999.67 (2nd consecutive record close). S&P 500 -0.57% to 7,511.35. Nasdaq -1.15% to 26,376.34. Chip stocks sharp decline; rotation into financials and industrials. Fed's first meeting under new Chair Kevin Warsh began June 16.

items.title: Hang Seng Index falls 1.4% to 24,494 as investors lock in gains
items.content: HSI closed at 24,493.95, reversing a two-day rally. Finance and tech weakness. Market awaiting China industrial production and retail sales data.

items.title: US core PCE inflation rises to 2.9% in May, highest since September 2025
items.content: Core PCE annual rate hit 2.9%, above Fed's 2% target. Energy prices surged: gasoline +7.0% in May, overall energy +3.9%. Shelter costs also increased. Next PCE release: June 25.

items.title: US and Iran reach initial ceasefire agreement to reopen Strait of Hormuz
items.content: MOU to be signed June 19 in Geneva. 60-day ceasefire extension, Iran allowed to resume oil sales. Oil prices dropped sharply. Strait reopening dependent on shipping companies' risk assessment.

items.title: China releases 2026-2028 three-year AI integration plan; stock indices rebalanced toward tech
items.content: MIIT three-year plan targets autonomous networks and wider AI applications by 2028. Major stock indices rebalanced June 15 to add more AI and semiconductor names, replacing traditional hardware/telecom stocks. China IC production +36% in Jan-Apr 2026.

items.title: Best Buy Q1 FY2027 beats: EPS $1.28 vs $1.23 est; revenue $8.94B
items.content: Best Buy Q1 revenue $8.94B (vs $8.77B YoY). Full-year guidance: adj EPS $6.30-$6.60. Consumer electronics demand stabilizing.

items.title: Snowflake Q1 FY2027: product revenue $1.334B, +34% YoY, beats estimates
items.content: EPS $0.39 vs $0.32 est. Full-year product revenue guidance $5.84B (+31% YoY). Cloud data demand remains strong.

items.title: Dollar Tree Q1 FY2026: adj EPS $1.74, +38% YoY, revenue $4.98B beats expectations
items.content: Net sales +7.2% YoY. Full-year adj EPS guidance $6.70-$7.10. Discount retail resilient despite inflation pressure.

items.title: FOMC two-day meeting begins under new Chair Kevin Warsh; rate decision due June 17
items.content: First FOMC meeting chaired by Kevin Warsh. Market pricing in rate hold. Focus on dot plot and forward guidance amid sticky inflation (core PCE 2.9%).

items.title: Oil prices plunge on US-Iran ceasefire deal; WTI drops below $60
items.content: Crude oil prices fell sharply as US-Iran agreement could reopen Strait of Hormuz, restoring disrupted supply routes. Energy crisis expected to take months to fully ease.

items.title: Hong Kong Q1 GDP +5.9% YoY; exports surge on AI-related electronics demand
items.content: Real GDP accelerated from 4.0% in Q4 2025. Export volume +32.3% in April. Full-year GDP forecast maintained at 2.5%-3.5%. Unemployment steady at 3.7%.

items.title: Strait of Hormuz deal brings fragile calm; full reopening dependent on Geneva signing and shipping confidence
items.content: Iran continues threats against commercial shipping. Mine-laying activities persist. Full reopening conditional on June 19 signing and shipping companies' willingness to resume transit.
